statement: The high-block source model satisfies the norm-convergence and spectral-gap hypotheses of the abstract Riesz projection theorem.

compactCompressionInputStatus.label: compact-compression norm convergence input
compactCompressionInputStatus.reason: The high-block compact-compression theorem gives norm convergence of compressed source operators.

sourceGapInputStatus.label: source spectral-gap input
sourceGapInputStatus.reason: The high-block source spectral-gap theorem gives an isolated active top-two cluster.

abstractSpectralInputStatus.label: abstract spectral projection input
abstractSpectralInputStatus.reason: The abstract Riesz theorem gives active/inactive spectral projection stability.

highBlockSpectralProjectionStatus.label: high-block spectral projection stability
highBlockSpectralProjectionStatus.reason: The high-block inputs satisfy the abstract spectral projection theorem.

proof: Use compact-compression norm convergence for the compressed source operators.
Use the interval-certified source spectral gap for the top-two active cluster.
Apply the abstract Riesz projection continuity theorem through the abstract compact-source spectral theorem.
